data.jd_text:
In a world of possibilities, pursue one with endless opportunities. Imagine Next!
  
  
At Parsons, you can imagine a career where you thrive, work with exceptional people, and be yourself. Guided by our leadership vision of valuing people, embracing agility, and fostering growth, we cultivate an innovative culture that empowers you to achieve your full potential. Unleash your talent and redefine what’s possible.
  
  
**Job Description**
**Assistant Project Manager (UAE National)**
**Dubai, UAE**
Parsons is looking for an amazingly talented
**Assistant Project Manager (UAE National)**
to join our team! In this role you will get to join our team of innovators! At Parsons Corporation, we're looking for an amazingly talented Assistant Project Manager to help us change the world. This is an opportunity to be part of a company that values your skills, encourages innovation, and believes in the power of collaboration..
  
  
**What You'll Be Doing**

* Manage and Control Project Communications: You'll be the central hub of information, ensuring clear and effective communication across all project stakeholders.
* Coordinate and Control Deliverables: You'll ensure that all project deliverables are completed on time and meet the highest quality standards.
* Projects' Quality Management: You'll oversee the quality of all project aspects, ensuring that we meet and exceed the expectations of our clients.
* Projects' Reporting: You'll be responsible for regular project reports, keeping everyone informed about the project's progress.
* Stakeholder Management: You'll work closely with all project stakeholders, ensuring their needs and expectations are met.
* Guidance: You'll provide guidance to the team working on the same project, fostering a collaborative and productive work environment.

**What Required Skills You'll Bring**

* Education: Bachelors degree in Business Administration or Engineering or Technology, in such areas as Civil, Electrical, or Mechanical.
* Experience: Fresh graduates with some internship experience are welcome to apply. More experienced candidates are also invited.
* Technical Skills: Working knowledge of AutoCAD and other PC software packages typically associated with engineering is required.
* Project Experience: Experience working on Infrastructure related projects is a plus.
* Must be UAE national.

Parsons equally employs representation at all job levels no matter the race, color, religion, sex (including pregnancy), national origin, age, disability or genetic information.
  
  
We truly invest and care about our employee’s wellbeing and provide endless growth opportunities as the sky is the limit, so aim for the stars! Imagine next and join the Parsons quest—APPLY TODAY!
  
  
Parsons is aware of fraudulent recruitment practices. To learn more about recruitment fraud and how to report it, please refer to https://www.parsons.com/fraudulent-recruitment/.

data.title: IT Program Delivery Support Manager
data.raw_snippet:
**Job Purpose**
To act as the central point of contact for support by area and regional IT Programs deployments. Provide direct local support for all implementations and deployments of DHL services. Provide regular reports to country IT Programs Delivery Manager on status of all projects. To work wi
data.jd_text:
**Job Purpose**
To act as the central point of contact for support by area and regional IT Programs deployments. Provide direct local support for all implementations and deployments of DHL services. Provide regular reports to country IT Programs Delivery Manager on status of all projects. To work with country IT Services Manager and business to ensure that adequate resources for both IT and business are available to support all agreed project plans. Provide support to Infrastructure Manager for facility and infrastructure projects as required
  
  
**RESPONSIBILITES**

* Working relationship with Country business teams, e.g. working with OPS, GTWY, FINANACE, CS & COMMERCIAL teams to deploy EGAP solutions and achieve functional must-do’s and KPIs.
* Working relationship with Regional Program Management to channel requirement and/or change requests and planning Country deployments.
* Working relationships with ITSC’s (and other service providers) to priorities projects, review impact of service performance on issues and requests raised by external or internal customers.
* Manage the project and report regularly against the project plan to ensure visibility of project progress.
* Develop project plans, scope, key stakeholder requirements.
* Monitor investment cost, ensure adequate resources are available.
* Identify project costs and benefits.
* Produce project plans that define the purpose, scope, deliverables and success criteria for each project. Obtain agreement to these plans from all project sponsors.
* Produce or draft a project budget.
* Assemble project teams as appropriate from internal staff and outside vendors.
* Manage the projects according to agreed plans and budgets.
* Identify risks that could impact the completion of the project within budget and on time.
* Escalate issues as appropriate and ensure resolution.
* Complete project “Life Cycle” and ensure project sign off.
* Carry out post project reviews to identify if the originally determined benefits have been realized and take corrective or remedial action as appropriate.
* Manage internal regional or global workgroups to deliver country projects.
* Define the scope and goals of projects to meet the Region or Country’s objectives with the IT Programs Delivery Manager.
* Standardize service portfolio by aligning services offered as per the EGAP portfolio and guidelines.
* Create awareness of solutions and services amongst country business units
* Productize appropriate solutions (as per global direction) to reduce the time to market.
* Optimize business and operations processes via automation
* Manage the project team to ensure the identified project methodology is adhered to and deliverables are achieved.
* Foster cooperation and a strong customer-focused approach within the project team.
* Develop a high-performance service culture within the project team.

**Requirements**

* Strong technical background and understanding of business and operational processes.
* Good knowledge of telecommunications and networking.
* Ability to direct and influence other senior managers outside the direct line management structure to undertake activities and achieve project objectives.
* Excellent spoken and written English communication skills.
* Excellent presentation and facilitation skills.
* Recognized industry-standard Project Management methodologies (e.g. Prince 2/PMP)
* At least 5 years of multi-disciplined IT experience including:
* Experience in a large, sophisticated organization with high transaction/volume environment including the physical shipment of goods.
* Exposures to an IT department delivering IT strategies that are tied to business goals.
* In depth understanding of international standards such as ISO and ITIL.
* Energetic, enthusiastic, results oriented and driven by goal achievement with a strong focus on excellence and high-quality project delivery.
* Degree in Information Services (or equivalent)

data.title: MCA Operational Performance Director
data.raw_snippet:
**Minimum Required Qualifications**
**Education:**

* Experience in testing, inspection, certification activities is a plus

data.jd_text:
**Minimum Required Qualifications**
**Education:**
Bachelor’s degree in engineering or a related technical discipline (Master’s preferred).
  
  
**Technical Knowledge**

* Experience in testing, inspection, certification activities is a plus
* Practical experience of lean methods, 5S, standardized work, problem solving, VSM, Kaizen, Hoshin Kanri with evidence of real sustainable financial and behavioral results
* Project management certificate or experience
* Advanced Office 365 skills

**Experience**
10+ years professional work experience, preferably in the testing, inspection, certification activities
  
  
**Role Purpose**
The Performance Manager drives business profitability across the Middle East, Caspian & Africa (MCA) region by deploying strategic process improvement initiatives and digital transformation programs. Leading a team of Area Performance Directors and support staff, this role executes modernization of operations, optimizes workflows, and establishes a culture of operational excellence that enhances client experience and Bureau Veritas' efficiency.
  
  
**Responsibilities**
**Strategic Performance & Profitability Leadership**

* Establish annual profitability targets for MCA countries and deploy prioritized process improvement projects to achieve group-level performance goals
* Partner with underperforming business units to diagnose operational challenges and implement corrective actions
* Monitor LEAP 28 TIQ and Performance indicators to track progress and drive sustained improvement

**Digital Transformation & Field Operations Modernization**

* Lead strategic deployment of digital systems to modernize and optimize field operations and workflows across the region
* Drive digitization of harmonized processes in partnership with the Digital Transformation (DxT) team
* Deploy Tableau digital cockpits and comprehensive TIQ/Performance KPI visualization tools
* Establish governance frameworks with HR, M&S, Finance, DxT, HSSE, and P&L leaders to optimize digital system adoption

**Process Improvement Program Execution**

* Establish and monitor process improvement projects across regions, areas, and countries
* Review operational and administrative processes using lean methodologies to eliminate waste and streamline workflows
* Identify alternatives (shared services centers, resource transfers, etc.) to optimize production capacity
* Harmonize and replicate best-practice processes across MCA for consistency and scalability
* Ensure end-to-end process traceability from opportunity identification through cash collection

**Change Leadership & Organizational Adoption**

* Direct Area Performance Directors and support team in executing improvement initiatives and monitoring adoption
* Drive alignment through clear communications on process changes and performance expectations
* Embed operational excellence as a day-to-day practice and champion continuous improvement

data.jd_text:
**Operations Director – AI Infrastructure**

**Location:**
Dubai, United Arab Emirates

**Industry:**
Data Center & AI Infrastructure

We are partnering with a fast-growing AI infrastructure company to appoint a highly capable
**Operations Director**
to oversee project execution, compliance, and high-level administrative operations across the UAE.

**Key Responsibilities:**

* **Project Execution & Vendor Coordination:**
  Lead the seamless coordination and management of external third parties, including data center design consultants, construction contractors, IT vendors, and local utility departments to ensure projects are delivered on time and to the highest standards.
* **Document Control & KYC Readiness:**
  Establish and maintain a rigorous document control system for both infrastructure projects and corporate operations. Systematically archive all third-party engineering contracts, government approvals, lease agreements, and financial invoices to ensure the company remains fully audit-ready for stringent KYC and due diligence processes conducted by global banks and sovereign wealth funds.
* **Entity Compliance & Government Relations:**
  Serve as the primary guardian of local entity compliance, managing day-to-day interactions with UAE Free Zones, municipal licensing departments, and regulatory bodies. Ensure all corporate licenses, land permits, and operational certifications remain current and fully compliant. Oversee local PRO services to efficiently handle visa, labor, and administrative requirements for the growing team and expatriate executives.
* **Administrative & Logistics Leadership:**
  Deliver high-end logistical support for visiting multinational board members, key investors, and government officials, including ground transportation, accommodation, and meeting arrangements that consistently meet executive standards.

**Requirements:**

* 7 to 10 years of hands-on experience working in the
  **UAE**
  as an Operations Director, Senior Project Manager, or PMO Lead within large enterprises, multinational tech firms, or heavy infrastructure sectors.
* Proven experience with
  **data center (IDC) build-outs**
  or complex local supply chain management is highly preferred.
* Strong execution mindset with the ability to take established plans and deliver results within complex local vendor networks and bureaucratic environments.
* Deep understanding of documentation standards, contract management, and
  **KYC/compliance**
  requirements in high-finance and heavy infrastructure projects — “if it is not documented, it did not happen.”
* Commanding professional presence with excellent problem-solving skills; able to hold external contractors accountable while navigating the unique operational challenges of the Middle East.
* **Language:**
  Native-level fluency in English (both spoken and written) for business communication and negotiation. Fluency in Arabic is considered a significant advantage.

**What’s on Offer:**

This is a critical leadership role within a high-profile AI infrastructure initiative in the UAE. You will play a pivotal part in ensuring operational excellence, regulatory compliance, and smooth execution as the company scales its sovereign AI computing capabilities in the region. The role reports directly to senior leadership.

If you are an experienced operations professional with strong UAE market knowledge, exceptional attention to detail, and a proven ability to manage complex infrastructure projects and compliance matters, we would like to hear from you.

data.title: Technical Program Manager, AMET 3PL and Direct Imports
data.raw_snippet:
**Description**
As a PM on the Supply Chain Automation team, you'll design and build full-stack applications and data-intensive systems that power critical supply chain decisions. You'll work with massive datasets, develop and implement science-based artifacts capable to impact broader supply chain,
data.jd_text:
**Description**
As a PM on the Supply Chain Automation team, you'll design and build full-stack applications and data-intensive systems that power critical supply chain decisions. You'll work with massive datasets, develop and implement science-based artifacts capable to impact broader supply chain, and create intuitive interfaces that enable our operations teams to work smarter and faster.
  
  
Key job responsibilities
  
  
Design, develop, and deploy full-stack applications for supply chain optimization and automation
  
  
Build scalable backend services using modern technologies (Java, Python, Node.js, etc.) on AWS infrastructure
  
  
Develop responsive, performant frontend applications using React, JavaScript/TypeScript, Redux, and modern web technologies
  
  
Architect and implement data pipelines leveraging our lakehouse architecture with Apache Iceberg
  
  
Work with large-scale data systems including Amazon Redshift, S3, and EMR
  
  
Perform data mining and analysis to uncover insights that drive supply chain improvements
  
  
Develop and support scientific solutions that are exemplary in terms of design, clarity, extensibility, and the ability to evolve over time
  
  
Innovate with the latest technology to build highly automated solutions for efficient forecasting and supply chain automation
  
  
Design, develop and deploy end-to-end machine learning solutions in the Amazon production environment to improvise the current supply chain through forecasting
  
  
Own the full software development lifecycle from design to deployment and operations
  
  
Participate in code reviews, design discussions, and contribute to technical strategy
  
  
**About The Team**
Join Amazon's Supply Chain Automation team, chartered with building the next generation of automation and optimization systems from the ground up. Our mission is to transform supply chain operations through intelligent automation, reducing manual effort while increasing speed, accuracy, and efficiency across Amazon's fulfillment network.
  
  
**Basic Qualifications**

* 3+ years of technical product or program management experience
* 2+ years of software development experience
* 3+ years of project management disciplines including scope, schedule, budget, quality, along with risk and critical path management experience
* Experience managing programs across cross functional teams, building processes and coordinating release schedules
* Bachelor's degree in engineering, computer science or equivalent
* Experience programming with at least one modern language such as Python, Ruby, Golang, Java, C++, C#, Rust
* Experience designing rich user interfaces using modern web frameworks (such as React, Angular, and Vue)
* Knowledge of object-oriented design, data structures, and algorithms
* Experience with modeling tools such as R, scikit-learn, Spark MLLib, MxNet, Tensorflow, numpy, scipy etc.
* Experience with scripting language (e.g., Python, Java, or R)
* Experience effectively gathering information from multiple data sources
* Experience architecting/operating solutions built on AWS

**Preferred Qualifications**

* 3+ years of working directly with engineering teams experience

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. data.jd_text:
**Description**
If you are a supply chain professional with a passion to innovate and contribute in a fast paced growing environment- you might be a good fit to join Ultra Fast grocery (UFG) Supply chain team as an S&OP Manager . The S&OP program manager acts as a bridge between business and operations teams and is responsible for seamless business planning and execution during peak and off peak periods while maximizing customer/seller experience. This role involves driving critical decisions with cross functional teams and senior leadership and demands strong program management skills along-with an ability to drive data driven discussions. The candidate is expected to have exceptional analytical, planning and written/verbal communication skills along with preferred experience in Forecasting, Sales & Operations Planning and Inventory management.
  
  
Key job responsibilities
  
  

* Set up robust processes to improve forecasting accuracy with an objective to minimize cost and maximize customer/seller experience
* Support the business growth in constrained environments with Frugal and innovative solutions
* Make the existing processes scalable by identifying and implementing available tools/automation
* Rollout best practices from other geographies/ companies to minimize end to end supply chain losses
* Utilize exceptional problem-solving and communication skills to influence business, operations and technical audiences
* Program manage and drive decision making across cross functional teams and senior leadership

**Basic Qualifications**

* 3+ years of program or project management experience
* 3+ years of working cross functionally with tech and non-tech teams experience
* 3+ years of defining and implementing process improvement initiatives using data and metrics experience
* Bachelor's degree
* Knowledge of Excel (Pivot Tables, VLookUps) at an advanced level and SQL
* Experience defining program requirements and using data and metrics to determine improvements

**Preferred Qualifications**

* 3+ years of driving end to end delivery, and communicating results to senior leadership experience
* 3+ years of driving process improvements experience
* Experience in stakeholder management, dealing with multiple stakeholders at varied levels of the organization
* Experience building processes, project management, and schedules

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. data.title: Principal Project Manager
data.raw_snippet:
**Who are we?**
At Finastra, we are a dynamic global provider of open finance software solutions, dedicated to expanding access to financial services. Our innovative applications span Lending, Payments, Treasury and Capital Markets, and Universal Banking. Proudly serving over 8,000 customers, includ
data.jd_text:
**Who are we?**
At Finastra, we are a dynamic global provider of open finance software solutions, dedicated to expanding access to financial services. Our innovative applications span Lending, Payments, Treasury and Capital Markets, and Universal Banking. Proudly serving over 8,000 customers, including 45 of the world's top 50 banks, we aim to boost financial inclusion for all. Join us and be part of a vibrant company that embraces diverse perspectives, and is committed to doing well by doing good.
  
  
**What will you contribute?**
**The Presales and Project Management Principal**
is responsible for ensuring that all projects within the portfolio are delivered on time, within the client's budget, and meet or exceed financial targets, including baseline margins. This role defines project objectives and oversees quality assurance throughout the project lifecycle. A key aspect of the role is to consistently foster strong client relationships and uphold Finastra’s reputation for excellence. Additionally, the Principal ensures that all presales proposals are submitted punctually and are aligned with budget expectations in coordination with Sales and the Client.
  
  
**Principal Project Manager**
is responsible for managing all aspects of Large to moderately complex projects for a defined segment of strategic initiatives from project definition and intake to transition to full operational support.
  
  
**Key Responsibilities**
Your deliverables as a
**Principal Project Manager**
will include, but are not limited to, the following
  
  

* Leads and drives the development of the project intake process including developing scope, schedule and cost
* Defines the goals and objectives of specific project and defines individual team member’s roles and responsibilities for the duration of the project
* Manages multiple mid to large scale projects ensuring that they will complete on time and within budget and that the solution aligns with/meets the business objective
* Manages the daily/weekly project team assignments and monitors progress against the work plan to assure that objectives are met within the prescribed time, cost, and quality measures
* Identification and impact analysis of potential project changes and facilitation of change control board to approve or disapprove said changes.
* Meets financial objectives by forecasting requirements; preparing a project budget; scheduling expenditures analyzing variances; initiating corrective actions
* Continual identification, qualification and tracking of risks, defining mitigation and contingency plans, assigning appropriate resources, and constantly communicating status
* Responsible for identification of resource requirements and proper communication of those requirements in a timely manner to ensure the project is properly staffed with the skilled resources required
* Responsible for identifying internal and external stakeholders, understanding their communication requirements and fulfilling those requirements

**Requirements And Qualifications**

* 15+ years of end-to-end Project Management experience
* Fluent in Arabic; native-level proficiency is highly preferred
* Experience must be with customer-facing projects and not internal to the organization
* Experience assisting project management teams with identification of action items, tasks and activities and tracking progress
* Experience at defining, implementing and continually improving standards, processes and procedures utilizing industry best practice (PMI, CMMI, etc.)
* Experience developing project budgets based on the development of Work Breakdown Structure (WBS)

**Benefits**
We are proud to offer a range of incentives to our employees worldwide. These benefits are available to everyone, regardless of grade, and reflect the values we uphold:
  
  

* Flexibility: Enjoy unlimited vacation, based on your location and business priorities. Hybrid working arrangements, and inclusive policies such as paid time off for voting, bereavement, and sick leave.
* Well-being: Access Confidential one-on-one therapy through our Employee Assistance Program, find support from our network of Wellbeing Champions and Gather Groups, and a calendar of monthly events and initiatives designed to help you thrive - Inside and Outside of work.
* Medical, life & disability insurance, retirement plan, lifestyle and other benefits\*
* ESG: Benefit from paid time off for volunteering and donation matching.
* DEI: Participate in multiple DE&I groups for open involvement (e.g., Count Me In, Culture@Finastra, Proud@Finastra, Disabilities@Finastra, Women@Finastra).
* Career Development: Access online learning and accredited courses through our Skills & Career Navigator tool.
* Recognition: Be part of our global recognition program, Finastra Celebrates, and contribute to regular employee surveys to help shape Finastra and foster a culture where everyone is engaged and empowered to perform at their best.
* Specific benefits may vary by location.

At Finastra, each individual is unique, bringing their own ideas, thoughts, cultural beliefs, backgrounds, and experiences together. We learn from one another, embrace and celebrate our differences, and create an environment where everyone feels safe to be themselves.
  
  
Be unique, be exceptional, and help us make a difference at Finastra!

data.title: Project Manager - ONESOURCE
data.raw_snippet:
**Project Manager – ONESOURCE**
**Thomson Reuters**
is a leading provider of transaction tax management solutions to companies of all sizes, enabling Finance, Tax and IT professionals to achieve accurate, timely and cost-effective compliance. Our flagship offering, the Thomson Reuters Application Su
data.jd_text:
**Project Manager – ONESOURCE**
**Thomson Reuters**
is a leading provider of transaction tax management solutions to companies of all sizes, enabling Finance, Tax and IT professionals to achieve accurate, timely and cost-effective compliance. Our flagship offering, the Thomson Reuters Application Suite, provides Consolidated Transaction Tax Management software and services, enabling global enterprise to manage sales, use, value-add, excise and specialty taxes with consistency, accuracy, control and efficiency. The marketplace is demanding our products, and Thomson Reuters is poised to grow significantly over the next several years in geographies across the world.
  
  
We are now hiring an
**ONESOURCE Technology Implementation & Consulting Project Manager**
to lead the delivery of software implementation projects within the Indirect Tax consulting organization. You will work closely with project teams, peer project managers, sales, customer service, clients, and partner consulting teams.
  
  
**About The Role**
As a
**ONESOURCE Project Manager,**
you will be responsible for:
  
  

* Service Delivery & Portfolio Management
* Manage an assigned portfolio of Thomson Reuters implementation projects.
* Direct project teams and own end-to-end delivery.
* Ensure strong planning, quality control, client management, and customer satisfaction.
* End-to-End Project Management
* Lead project planning sessions to define scope, approach, and delivery plans.
* Manage multiple complex projects/issues simultaneously, often requiring subject matter expertise.
* Drive operational improvements, efficiency, and results.
* Meet revenue and utilization targets as defined by management.
* Communication & Status Reporting
* Maintain clear communication with clients and internal teams.
* Prepare and deliver regular project status updates.
* Share knowledge and updates across the broader organization.
* Delivery Leadership
* Provide guidance and leadership to team members (and/or manage a team).
* Make routine decisions within established guidelines, including approved exceptions.
* Manage or contribute to project cost control, forecasting, and budget recommendations.
* Ensure quality and timeliness of deliverables; maintain accountability across the team.
* Provide insights and recommendations to support broader organizational goals.
* Consulting & Subject Matter Expertise
* Deliver consulting services to external clients in tax technology, legal, and global trade domain as part of Thomson Reuters Professional service team.
* Provide subject matter expertise to support customer deliverables.
* Travel
* 40–45% travel is required.

**About You**
To be our
**ONESOURCE Project Manager**
, you will likely have:
  
  

* 12+ years of core project management experience (with project management certification e.g., PMP, PRINCE2, or equivalent).
* 15+ years total experience in ERP, tax, or technology domains.
* Experience delivering consulting and technology implementation projects with diverse teams and large customers, including MENA and global clients.
* Proven ability to manage multiple parallel projects end-to-end.
* Strong understanding of:

+ O2C (Order-to-Cash) and/or P2P (Procure-to-Pay)
+ Indirect tax, e-invoicing, or related tax technology processes

* Background in tax technology or professional services organizations (e.g., systems integrators, ERP consulting firms, Big 4), ideally supporting large enterprises.
* Strong communication and coordination skills, including stakeholder management with senior client leadership, partners, and consultants.
* Fluent English (Arabic is a plus).

**Highly Desired**

* Integration/implementation experience with enterprise tax products such as ONESOURCE Tax Determination, Pagero e-invoicing, Taxware, Vertex, Avalara
* Experience with implementation and rollout of large ERP programs and/or Tax transformation and/or E-invoice solution implementation project management.

**What’s in it For You?**

* Hybrid Work Model: We’ve adopted a flexible hybrid working environment (2-3 days a week in the office depending on the role) for our office-based roles while delivering a seamless experience that is digitally and physically connected.
* Flexibility & Work-Life Balance: Flex My Way is a set of supportive workplace policies designed to help manage personal and professional responsibilities, whether caring for family, giving back to the community, or finding time to refresh and reset. This builds upon our flexible work arrangements, including work from anywhere for up to 8 weeks per year, empowering employees to achieve a better work-life balance.
* Career Development and Growth: By fostering a culture of continuous learning and skill development, we prepare our talent to tackle tomorrow’s challenges and deliver real-world solutions. Our Grow My Way programming and skills-first approach ensures you have the tools and knowledge to grow, lead, and thrive in an AI-enabled future.
* Industry Competitive Benefits: We offer comprehensive benefit plans to include flexible vacation, two company-wide Mental Health Days off, access to the Headspace app, retirement savings, tuition reimbursement, employee incentive programs, and resources for mental, physical, and financial wellbeing.
* Culture: Globally recognized, award-winning reputation for inclusion and belonging, flexibility, work-life balance, and more. We live by our values: Obsess over our Customers, Compete to Win, Challenge (Y)our Thinking, Act Fast / Learn Fast, and Stronger Together.
* Social Impact: Make an impact in your community with our Social Impact Institute. We offer employees two paid volunteer days off annually and opportunities to get involved with pro-bono consulting projects and Environmental, Social, and Governance (ESG) initiatives.
* Making a Real-World Impact: We are one of the few companies globally that helps its customers pursue justice, truth, and transparency. Together, with the professionals and institutions we serve, we help uphold the rule of law, turn the wheels of commerce, catch bad actors, report the facts, and provide trusted, unbiased information to people all over the world.

**About Us**
Thomson Reuters informs the way forward by bringing together the trusted content and technology that people and organizations need to make the right decisions. We serve professionals across legal, tax, accounting, compliance, government, and media. Our products combine highly specialized software and insights to empower professionals with the data, intelligence, and solutions needed to make informed decisions, and to help institutions in their pursuit of justice, truth, and transparency. Reuters, part of Thomson Reuters, is a world leading provider of trusted journalism and news.
  
  
We are powered by the talents of 26,000 employees across more than 70 countries, where everyone has a chance to contribute and grow professionally in flexible work environments. At a time when objectivity, accuracy, fairness, and transparency are under attack, we consider it our duty to pursue them. Sound exciting? Join us and help shape the industries that move society forward.
  
  
As a global business, we rely on the unique backgrounds, perspectives, and experiences of all employees to deliver on our business goals. To ensure we can do that, we seek talented, qualified employees in all our operations around the world regardless of race, color, sex/gender, including pregnancy, gender identity and expression, national origin, religion, sexual orientation, disability, age, marital status, citizen status, veteran status, or any other protected classification under applicable law. Thomson Reuters is proud to be an Equal Employment Opportunity Employer providing a drug-free workplace.
  
  
We also make reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities and for sincerely held religious beliefs in accordance with applicable law. More information on requesting an accommodation here.
  
  
Learn more on how to protect yourself from fraudulent job postings here.
  
  
More information about Thomson Reuters can be found on thomsonreuters.com.

Network Losses (NL) Organization exists to reduce and control operationally controllable defects that results into financial loss to the company presented in inventory overwrite or customer concessions.
  
  
NL AM -Network losses Area Manager- will be responsible for reducing operations defects leading to network losses.
  
  
NL AM will work closely & daily with field operations, security and loss prevention, legal, vendor management teams with to make sure process defects leading to losses are eliminated and reflected into goodness is reflected at weekly loss figures.
  
  
Key job responsibilities
  
  
Accountable for achieving the overarching Network Losses (NL) metrics targets through:
  
  

* Collaborating with operational stakeholders to align new input metrics.
* Daily tracking of input metrics, actively engaging with operations to implement fixes and enhance these metrics.
* Identifying and addressing new operational defects, implementing effective countermeasures.
* Identifying and highlighting structural challenges to drive the NL charter forward.
* Drive NL Weekly business review.
* Daily useage of SQL to generate business insights

**Basic Qualifications**

* 1+ years of employee and performance management experience
* Bachelor's degree or equivalent, or 2+ years of Amazon (blue badge/FTE) experience
* Work a flexible schedule/shift/work area, including weekends, nights, and/or holidays
* Can push and pull wheeled dollies loaded with products up to 100 pounds during shifts
* Experience with advanced use of SQL for data mining and business intelligence
* 6+ years of performance metrics, process improvement or lean techniques experience
* Six Sigma Black Belt Certification

**Preferred Qualifications**

data.title: Lead Data Intelligence Project Manager
data.raw_snippet:
**About Us**
At Dyson, we’re driven by a relentless pursuit of innovation—pushing boundaries in engineering, AI, and robotics. Our new Data Intelligence team sits at the heart of this mission: shaping Dyson’s future through data. Here, we blend creativity, precision, and audacity to power intelligen
data.jd_text:
**About Us**
At Dyson, we’re driven by a relentless pursuit of innovation—pushing boundaries in engineering, AI, and robotics. Our new Data Intelligence team sits at the heart of this mission: shaping Dyson’s future through data. Here, we blend creativity, precision, and audacity to power intelligent products. We craft data strategies and pipelines that fuel the next generation of connected devices.
  
  
You’ll work alongside brilliant minds from Dyson global engineering team and external software/hardware partners in an environment built for exploration, discovery, delivery and impact.
  
  
**About The Role**
As the Lead Data Intelligence Project Manager, you ensure that every project has the resources it needs to take off, you'll orchestrate data operations that power product development across Dyson’s global teams. Key responsibilities include:
  
  

* Act as the primary liaison between our external project teams and our internal technical departments (Data Curation and MLOps). You will own the "data logistics" for the entire project lifecycle—from initial storage setup to final delivery of high-quality training sets.
* You will be the strategic lead for our labelling workforce, managing the balance between our in-house contractors and 3rd party service providers to ensure we never hit a capacity bottleneck.
* Overseeing data storage setup and maintenance to guarantee security, accessibility, and compliance.
* Maintaining rigorous documentation and workflows that ensure project transparency and repeatability.
* Supporting cross-functional communication between data teams, contractors, and engineering partners.

**About you**

* 8+ years in Project or Program Management, with at least 2 years specifically in AI/ML Data Operations or Data Management. You’ve delivered complex projects on time and within scope—often in fast-changing settings.
* Demonstrated success in managing 3rd party vendors or service providers, including contract negotiation, quality assurance, issue resolution, and ongoing relationship management.
* Machine Learning Lifecycle Knowledge: Strong understanding of how data flows from collection → cleaning → labeling → training → deployment.
* Tooling Familiarity: Experience with labeling platforms (Labelbox, CVAT, Label Studio) and project management tools (Jira, Asana, or Airtable).
* Data Literacy: Basic understanding of data storage architectures (S3, SQL, Snowflake) and data privacy regulations (GDPR, CCPA).
* Communication: Exceptional "translation" skills—the ability to explain technical MLOps constraints to business stakeholders and vice versa.
* A solid understanding of modern data storage solutions (cloud, on-premises, hybrid), including principles of data security, privacy, access control, and regulatory compliance.
* Able to translate complex technical requirements into clear actions for both technical and non-technical audiences. You’re adept at influencing and building consensus.
* Confident interfacing with cross-functional teams—coordinating with data scientists, engineers, product managers, and external stakeholders to deliver smooth handovers and alignment.
* Well-versed in project management tools and methodologies (e.g., Agile, Scrum, or Waterfall). Known for driving continuous improvement, clear documentation, and repeatable processes.
* Thorough, highly organized, and relentless in troubleshooting issues and removing blockers before they impact delivery.
* Proactive, accountable, and comfortable juggling competing priorities. You always keep end goals in focus and balance urgency with quality.
* Bachelor’s degree in a relevant discipline (e.g., Computer Science, Data Engineering, Engineering, or Information Technology); higher degree or PMP/Prince2 certification is a plus.

Dyson is an equal opportunity employer. We know that great minds don’t think alike, and it takes all kinds of minds to make our technology so unique. We welcome applications from all backgrounds and employment decisions are made without regard to race, colour, religion, national or ethnic origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, age, disability, protected veteran status or other any other dimension of diversity.

data.title: Technical Program Engineer - Manager / Scrum Master - Global Data Center Segment
data.raw_snippet:
Eaton has been a key player in the Middle East for 40 years. Last year, we announced the construction of a
**new sustainable campus in Dubai,**
which will bring together our commercial, manufacturing, and support functions, providing room for future growth.
  
  
Eaton's Global Data Center Segment i
data.jd_text:
Eaton has been a key player in the Middle East for 40 years. Last year, we announced the construction of a
**new sustainable campus in Dubai,**
which will bring together our commercial, manufacturing, and support functions, providing room for future growth.
  
  
Eaton's Global Data Center Segment is focused on helping data centers realize real business benefits today while optimizing their operations for the future. With the rise of big data, edge computing and the cloud, data centers are under pressure to be more high-performing, sustainable and cost-effective than ever, without compromising resilience or security. At the same time, new opportunities and revenue streams require faster speed-to-market. With our digital intelligence, world-class cyber-secure products and services, reliable, compatible solutions and engineering expertise, we help data centers realize real business benefits today while optimizing their operations for the future.
  
  
Within this segment, Eaton has an opening for a
**Technical Program Manager/Scrum Master – Global Data Center Segment.**
The primary function of this role is to act as an experienced servant leader and Agile coach for cross functional Datacenter teams within Eaton’s Global Data Center Segment team that is based in UAE. This role enables teams delivering Data Center solutions to operate effectively using Scrum, accelerating value delivery while maintaining Eaton’s high standards for safety, quality, compliance, and reliability. Operating in a global, matrixed environment, the Scrum Master partners closely with Research and Development, Product Owners, engineering, compliance, and business stakeholders to remove impediments, reinforce Agile discipline, and support Eaton’s broader Data Center growth strategy.
  
  
**What You’ll Do**
Scrum & Agile Facilitation
  
  

* Serve as Scrum Master for one or more Data Center Teams, or solution teams, ensuring consistent application of Scrum events, roles, and artifacts in alignment with Eaton’s Scrum framework.
* Facilitate Sprint Planning, Daily Scrums, Sprint Reviews, and Retrospectives, fostering transparency, focus, and continuous improvement.
* Coach teams on Agile principles, self management, and cross functional collaboration.

Impediment Removal & Flow Optimization
  
  

* Proactively identify and remove delivery impediments, including dependencies across engineering, testing, UL compliance, manufacturing, and supply chain partners.
* Partner with leaders and stakeholders to escalate and resolve systemic issues impacting team flow and predictability.

Product & Stakeholder Partnership
  
  

* Support Product Owners in backlog refinement, prioritization, and alignment to Global Data Center Segment objectives, including safety, scalability, and reliability expectations.
* Act as a bridge between technical teams and global stakeholders, enabling clear communication and shared understanding of delivery commitments.

Metrics & Continuous Improvement
  
  

* Promote the use of Agile metrics (e.g., throughput, predictability, sprint goals) to enable data driven improvement without fostering a command and control culture.
* Reinforce Kaizen and continuous improvement practices aligned with Eaton’s Continuous Improvement System.

Scaling & Enterprise Agility
  
  

* Support coordination across multiple teams or workstreams contributing to Gray Space UL deliverables.
* Contribute to broader Agile maturity and ways of working improvements across the Global Data Center organization, consistent with Eaton’s global operating model

**Qualifications**

* Strong working knowledge of Scrum and Agile principles, with demonstrated ability to apply them pragmatically in enterprise settings.
* Experience working with the data center or IT industry at senior levels.
* Experience working with cross functional global teams, including engineering, compliance, and operations.
* Proven ability to influence without authority in a matrixed organization.
* Bachelor’s degree in any discipline from an accredited institution.

**Skills**

* Scrum Master certification (CSM, PSM, SAFe SM, or equivalent) preferred.
* Experience supporting data centers, power systems, electrical infrastructure, or regulated product development preferred.
* Familiarity with UL certification processes or safety‑critical product development.
* Experience operating within large, global enterprises.

**What We Offer You**

* Being part of a company that has been in business for more than 100 years, is well known in the industry, and has a major impact on the energy industry
* Annual mentoring program, Eaton University, a reward, and recognition system
* Ongoing Learning and Career Development Opportunities in a global company. Build your career and apply internally for our open positions worldwide
* Strong, agile and diverse team locally and globally
* Strong processes and state-of-the-art systems and tools
* Detailed induction support and well-structured onboarding
* Balance your work and life with a hybrid worktime model

**Living and Working in Dubai**
*Dubai is a vibrant, cosmopolitan city known for its modern architecture, luxurious lifestyle, and rich cultural heritage. Living and working in Dubai offers an exceptional quality of life with world-class amenities, a safe environment, and a thriving expatriate community. Experience a dynamic blend of work and leisure in one of the world’s most exciting cities.
  
  
Ready to make a difference? Join Eaton’s Dubai Team and be part of a company shaping the future of sustainable manufacturing and power management!*
We are committed to ensuring equal employment opportunities for job applicants and employees. Our recruitment processes use balanced selection criteria and avoid unlawful discrimination against applicants on the basis of their age, colour, disability, marital status, national origin, gender, gender identity, genetic information, race or racial origin, religion, sexual orientation or any other status protected or required by law.

Interested in working on an innovative and high-impact business that is changing the way customers think about grocery? The MENA Grocery team (UFG) is offering a role to own building new business from ground Zero. We are seeking a self-starter who can quickly familiarize themselves w
data.jd_text:
**Description**
Interested in working on an innovative and high-impact business that is changing the way customers think about grocery? The MENA Grocery team (UFG) is offering a role to own building new business from ground Zero. We are seeking a self-starter who can quickly familiarize themselves with this fast-paced environment, build relationships/trust with cross-functional teams, and assume ownership within the program which will require understanding of complex business/Tech/Ops processes at Amazon and our partners and devising an optimal engagement model. You must be able to thrive and succeed in an entrepreneurial environment, and not be hindered by ambiguity or competing priorities. High judgment, analytical talent and good document writing skills with concise communication are essential to success in this role.
  
  
In this role, You will influence internal and external stakeholders and leadership to buy in to your strategic direction in forging new business partnerships, drive program end to end to onboard partners while ensuring flawless execution and continuously analyzing key metrics to drive improvements for your business.
  
  
This position involves regular communication with senior leadership on Business and customer offering reviews, Operations excellence improvement areas, project execution and risks. You should be comfortable working both at a strategic level and diving deep into the details to ensure solution feasibility and quality of execution.
  
  
Key job responsibilities
  
  
Partner Onboarding & Merchant Launch
  
  

* Lead end-to-end partner onboarding programs, including new merchant acquisition, integration, and launch
* Manage merchant expansion initiatives to scale from current operations to 5+ merchants per country
* Coordinate with cross-functional teams to ensure successful merchant launches and ongoing relationship management
* Own the merchant onboarding lifecycle, ensuring timely delivery of all capabilities and requirements

Contracts & Legal Matters
  
  

* Partner with Legal teams to negotiate and execute partnership agreements, including Master Service Agreements (MSAs), addendums, and heads of terms
* Manage contract lifecycle from drafting through signature, ensuring compliance with company policies and financial requirements
* Coordinate contract reviews, approvals, and amendments with internal stakeholders and external partners
* Ensure all legal documentation is properly stored and tracked in Contract Central

PMO & Technical Program Management
  
  

* Build and manage PMO project schedules, plans, and metrics to support program delivery

New Initiatives & Programs
  
  

* Scope and deliver complex, business-critical programs end-to-end, driving overall program strategy and roadmap
* Define business and systems requirements working with internal and external stakeholders
* Lead strategic initiatives such as new market launches, service expansions, and operational improvements
* Develop and execute project plans from design to release, securing resources and managing implementation

**Basic Qualifications**

* 5+ years of program or project management experience
* 3+ years of working cross functionally with tech and non-tech teams experience
* Experience implementing repeatable processes and driving automation or standardization
* Knowledge of Excel at an intermediate level (e.g., pivot tables & charts, multiple criteria lookups, nested logical/IF formulas, data cleansing, array formulas, etc.)
* Experience defining program requirements and using data and metrics to determine improvements

**Preferred Qualifications**

* Experience in stakeholder management, dealing with multiple stakeholders at varied levels of the organization
* Experience building processes, project management, and schedules
* Experience defining and executing program requirements
* Experience in complex problem solving, and working in a tight schedule environment

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.
  
  
  
**Company**
- Q-Express Documents Transport

data.jd_text:

- Manage real-time data streaming pipelines via Confluent Kafka.

- Ensure alignment with data governance, security, and regulatory frameworks (e.g., GDPR, CBUAE, BCBS 239).

data.title: Project Controls and Claims Manager - 100% (m/f)
data.raw_snippet:
ARX is a global network of specialists in engineering, design, and architecture. Our team offers 360° consulting, project management, and technical services in:
  
  
airports, bridges, buildings, cableways,
  
  
digital innovation, environment, equipment,
  
  
geology, geotechnics, hydropower, me
data.jd_text:
ARX is a global network of specialists in engineering, design, and architecture. Our team offers 360° consulting, project management, and technical services in:
  
  
airports, bridges, buildings, cableways,
  
  
digital innovation, environment, equipment,
  
  
geology, geotechnics, hydropower, metros,
  
  
nuclear plants, oil & gas, pipelines, ports,
  
  
railways, river engineering, roads, traffic,
  
  
tunnels, and water/wastewater treatment.
  
  
With offices across Europe, North and South America, Asia, Africa, and Oceania, our agile task force combines global expertise with local know-how. The result is our uniquely
**"glocal"**
approach, which allows us to meet the specific needs of each community while incorporating international best practices. At ARX, smart minds strive for a sustainable future, transforming communities one innovative project at a time.
  
  
People are the heart and soul of ARX. We are a home for innovators, visionaries, and experts to develop talents, launch careers, and collaborate with fellow specialists. ARX values individuals. Believing their brilliance and determination will provide solutions to tomorrow's challenges, we welcome professionals who will both benefit from and enrich our team.
  
  
ARX, marks a significant expansion into the Middle East with its offices in UAE and KSA. This underscores our dedication to innovation and engineering excellence, bolstering our position in the dynamic GCC market.
  
  
For our office in Dubai, we are looking for
  
  
**Project Controls and Claims Manager**
- 100% (m/f)
  
  
The ideal candidate will have
**12-15 years of experience**
in project controls, planning, delay analysis, and claims management on major infrastructure and EPC projects. The Project Control Manager will be responsible for leading project controls activities across the full project lifecycle, including planning, scheduling, progress monitoring, reporting, cost and performance tracking, and support on contractual and claims-related matters. The role requires close coordination with project, commercial, and technical teams to ensure robust programme management, effective reporting, and timely identification of project risks and mitigation measures.
  
  
**Responsibilities:**

* Lead the development, maintenance, and monitoring of baseline master programmes and detailed project schedules using Primavera P6.
* Oversee project controls processes, including planning, progress measurement, reporting, forecasting, and performance analysis.
* Lead the preparation of lookaheads, recovery programmes, and what-if scenario analyses to support project delivery and decision-making.
* Monitor project progress against approved baselines, key milestones, and contractual obligations, and drive corrective actions where required.
* Coordinate with engineering, procurement, construction, and commercial teams to validate progress, assess impacts, and ensure alignment across all project functions.
* Prepare and review weekly and monthly progress reports, dashboards, S-curves, delay logs, and management summaries.
* Oversee the development and maintenance of project control tools, work breakdown structures, reporting frameworks, and progress tracking systems.
* Support cost-loaded programme development and integration between schedule, cost, and performance reporting.
* Lead or support earned value analysis, forecasting, and performance monitoring through key project indicators.
* Manage and support the preparation and review of extension of time claims, Time Impact Analyses, delay analyses, and associated planning narratives.
* Provide planning and contractual support in relation to project changes, delays, risk events, and claims strategy.
* Support tender and pre-contract planning activities, including preparation of master programmes, phasing plans, methodology-linked schedules, permit schedules, and indirect cost inputs.
* Lead coordination with clients, consultants, subcontractors, and internal stakeholders on programme compliance, reporting, schedule updates, and recovery planning.
* Provide guidance and direction to planners or junior project controls team members as required.

**Requirements:**

* Bachelor's degree in Engineering or a related discipline.
* 12-15 years of experience in project controls, planning, and claims management on large-scale infrastructure or EPC projects.
* Proven experience leading project controls functions on complex multidisciplinary projects.
* Strong experience in project scheduling, progress monitoring, forecasting, delay analysis, and management reporting.
* Proven involvement in extension of time claims and contractual planning matters.
* Experience across major infrastructure sectors such as rail, tunnelling, underground works, water, energy, or multidisciplinary civil projects.
* GCC experience is preferred.
* Strong command of Primavera P6 is mandatory.
* Good knowledge of project controls systems, reporting tools, and advanced Excel-based reporting.
* Sound understanding of FIDIC, NEC, or similar forms of contract.
* Strong communication, coordination, and stakeholder management skills.

**Competencies:**

* Strong leadership in project controls, with the ability to manage schedules, reporting, risks, and performance across complex projects.
* Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to identify trends, risks, delays, and required mitigation measures.
* Good commercial and contractual awareness, with the ability to support claims strategy and entitlement assessment.
* Able to work effectively across multidisciplinary teams and manage interfaces with internal and external stakeholders.
* Delivery-focused, structured, and proactive in managing competing priorities and deadlines.

**We Offer:**

* Opportunity to collaborate with a forward-thinking, synergistic, and innovative team.
* Exposure to cutting-edge projects driving Middle East / North African regions development.
* Personalized professional growth and developmental avenues tailored to your career aspirations.

If you would you like to join our highly motivated and very successful team, please send us your complete application!
  
  
Sector: Construction/Civil Engineering
  
  
Role: Engineering
  
  
Will manage other people: No
  
  
Job type: Permanent job
  
  
Career level: Employee

data.title: Senior Program Manager, Network Health / Trust & Safety
data.raw_snippet:
**Description**
This role is for someone who wants real ownership.
  
  
You’ll be the right hand to the Network Health and Trust & Safety leadership, owning complex, high-impact programs that protect customers, partners, and the business. This is not a reporting or coordination role. You will ident
data.jd_text:
**Description**
This role is for someone who wants real ownership.
  
  
You’ll be the right hand to the Network Health and Trust & Safety leadership, owning complex, high-impact programs that protect customers, partners, and the business. This is not a reporting or coordination role. You will identify risks before they escalate, turn ambiguity into clear execution plans, and drive programs that balance safety, compliance, and operational reality.
  
  
You’ll work across teams that don’t naturally align, often without direct authority. Your job is to connect the dots, make hard calls, and ensure the right things get done on time, at scale, and with measurable impact.
  
  
If you enjoy solving messy problems, influencing senior stakeholders, and building mechanisms that last, this role will stretch you in the best way.
  
  
Key job responsibilities
  
  

* Own end-to-end Trust & Safety and Network Health programs from problem definition to execution and sustained outcomes
* Identify systemic risks and compliance gaps; design scalable solutions rather than one-off fixes
* Drive execution across multiple teams with competing priorities
* Set clear goals, milestones, and success metrics for complex initiatives
* Act as a thought partner to senior leadership on risk, safety, and long-term strategy
* Build simple, durable mechanisms to track progress and enforce standards
* Raise the bar on how programs are designed, reviewed, and communicated

A day in the life
  
  
**On a Typical Day, You Might**

* Deep dive into data to understand where risks are emerging and why
* Align with product, operations, legal, and analytics partners on priorities and trade-offs
* Challenge unclear plans and push for sharper problem statements and success metrics
* Review program progress, unblock teams, and escalate when needed
* Translate complex issues into clear updates for senior stakeholders
* Improve existing processes so teams spend less time reacting and more time preventing issues

Your internal stakeholders include operations leaders, product teams, analytics partners, and legal or policy counterparts.
  
  
**About The Team**
The Network Health (NH) and Trust & Safety (T&S) teams work together to protect Amazon’s Last Mile operations by driving compliance, partner accountability, and safety across the AMET region (UAE, Saudi Arabia, Egypt, and Turkey).
  
  
Network Health owns the end-to-end compliance and enforcement lifecycle for Delivery Service Partners (DSPs) — from risk identification and MEP (Monitoring & Enforcement Plan) management to exit governance and performance reviews. The team ensures that all DSPs operate safely, ethically, and in alignment with Amazon’s Supply Chain Standards and local labor regulations.
  
  
Trust & Safety complements this work by focusing on driver-level safety, identity verification, and incident resolution programs such as ID Check, APEA, and DAF, ensuring that both DSPs and drivers uphold Amazon’s zero-tolerance standards on compliance and worker welfare.
  
  
This role operates at the intersection of both functions, helping deliver secure, compliant, and sustainable operations in one of Amazon’s fastest-growing regions.
  
  
**Basic Qualifications**

* 5+ years of program or project management experience
* Experience using data and metrics to determine and drive improvements
* Experience owning program strategy, end to end delivery, and communicating results to senior leadership
* Knowledge of Excel at an intermediate level (e.g., pivot tables & charts, multiple criteria lookups, nested logical/IF formulas, data cleansing, array formulas, etc.)

**Preferred Qualifications**

* 2+ years of driving process improvements experience
* Master's degree, or MBA in business, operations, human resources, adult education, organizational development, instructional design or related field

Our inclusive culture empowers Amazonians to deliver the best results for our customers. If you have a disability and need a workplace accommodation or adjustment during the application and hiring process, including support for the interview or onboarding process, please visit https://amazon.jobs/content/en/how-we-hire/accommodations for more information. If the country/region you’re applying in isn’t listed, please contact your Recruiting Partner.
  
  
  
**Company**
- Amazon Middle East and North Africa FZ-LLC
  
  
Job ID: A3176892

We are trusted by 300+ million people in 100+ countries for our industry-leading security, user fund transparency, trading engine speed, deep liquidity, and an
data.jd_text:
Binance is a leading global blockchain ecosystem behind the world’s largest cryptocurrency exchange by trading volume and registered users. We are trusted by 300+ million people in 100+ countries for our industry-leading security, user fund transparency, trading engine speed, deep liquidity, and an unmatched portfolio of digital-asset products. Binance offerings range from trading and finance to education, research, payments, institutional services, Web3 features, and more. We leverage the power of digital assets and blockchain to build an inclusive financial ecosystem to advance the freedom of money and improve financial access for people around the world.
  
  
**Responsibilities**

* Develop strong relationships with the project community, crypto founders and top VCs
* Proactively source and identify new blockchain and cryptocurrency projects for listing on Binance.com and Binance Alpha features
* Ensure a strong and continuous pipeline of solid projects for listing
* Provide your input on the due diligence process, helping the team evaluate new opportunities via team strength, competitive analysis, and tokenomics, among others
* Drive internal approval processes and present listing rationale to senior leaders of the organization
* Collaborate across teams to ensure coordinated efforts for deal sourcing and terms negotiation
* Attend relevant global events and represent Binance

**Requirements**

* In-depth understanding & relevant experience in the cryptocurrency and blockchain industry, including strong grasp of trends, themes, tokenomics, etc.
* Demonstrable passion for cryptocurrency and blockchain
* 8-10 years of relevant asset listing experience in crypto business/venture capital/ exchanges
* Strong written and analytical skills for deal analysis and internal reporting
* Strong interpersonal skills as the Corporate Development team meets with entrepreneurs and other members of the startup community on a regular basis
* Ability to work well within a team and to be self-guided on a day-to-day basis

**Why Binance**

* Shape the future with the world’s leading blockchain ecosystem
* Collaborate with world-class talent in a user-centric global organization with a flat structure
* Tackle unique, fast-paced projects with autonomy in an innovative environment
* Thrive in a results-driven workplace with opportunities for career growth and continuous learning
* Competitive salary and company benefits
* Work-from-home arrangement (the arrangement may vary depending on the work nature of the business team)

Binance is committed to being an equal opportunity employer. We believe that having a diverse workforce is fundamental to our success.
  
  
*By submitting a job application, you confirm that you have read and agree to our*
***Candidate Privacy Notice***
*.*

These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans.
