data.title: Senior Manager, Project Controls
data.raw_snippet:
**Building on our past. Ready for the future**
Worley is a global professional services company of energy, chemicals and resources experts. We partner with customers to deliver projects and create value over the life of their assets. We’re bridging two worlds, moving towards more sustainable energy
data.jd_text:
**Building on our past. Ready for the future**
Worley is a global professional services company of energy, chemicals and resources experts. We partner with customers to deliver projects and create value over the life of their assets. We’re bridging two worlds, moving towards more sustainable energy sources, while helping to provide the energy, chemicals and resources needed now.
  
  
**Primary Objective**

* This position is permanently assigned to the projects with a proven track record of the initiation of meaningful project control in a multi projects environment.

**The Responsibilities Of This Position Revolve Around**

* Manpower planning
* Cost estimating
* Project Management Plan preparation
* Client financial approvals documentation
* Cost control engineering
* Project scheduling
* Change management
* Corporate and Client cost and schedule progress reporting.
* Managing the document control system
* Risk management reviews

**Specific Accountabilities**

* You are expected to assure provision of full project controls such as planning/scheduling, estimation, cost control services for the assigned projects
* You will be required to prepare and issue a comprehensive Monthly Project Progress Report with requisite analysis of status.
* You will be responsible for the provision of reports required under the contract.
* You are required to ensure that the assigned projects are executed per the approved PMP's with respect to man-hours, cost, and quality, scope of work and time schedule.
* You will be tasked with identifying areas of concern with respect to progress of assigned projects and recommending the corrective action wherever required.
* You will be responsible for identification and recruitment of project controls personnel.
* Implementation of suitable systems, processes, tools, and procedures.

**Functional management**

* You are responsible for sourcing, development and mentoring of project controls specialists.
* You will hold custodianship of project controls systems, processes and tools.
* You are responsible for reviewing, auditing and improving project controls activities on specific projects
* You will be required to demonstrate the project controls capability in the areas of cost control and scheduling to existing and potential clients (business development)
* You are expected to be directly involved in execution of key projects which includes Proposal preparation, Team coaching & sponsorship

**Personality**

* You are an individual who is self-motivated, proactive, and works well with a team.
* You will be seen as the Subject matter specialist for the resolution of issues arising across all areas of the job and be able to clearly explain complex processes that surround the role.
* We expect you to ensure the integrity of the work scope and that all work is carried-out in accordance with applicable codes and standards.
* We expect you to mentor of less experienced members of staff including ‘hands on’ approach to the formulation and delivery of training programs for local Kuwaiti personnel.

**Preferred Qualification(s) And/or Experience**

* You will possess an engineering degree from an accredited college or university. This is an essential contractual requirement.
* You must have work experience within PMC projects in the above-mentioned industries.
* You will have experience working with advanced engineering tools, data systems & other general office / engineering software will be given first preference.
* You will have the ability to interact effectively with people of all nationalities & cultures while using clear, fluent, and expressive verbal and written language to convey information and ideas. An individual with excellent communication and people skills, a consummate developer of relationship(s) on a sustainable and long-term basis.
* You will have broad based knowledge of the requirements for process engineering through all phases of project life cycle, from FEED through to Commissioning, within Oil & Gas or related industries.

As Fitout Projects Manager is responsible for :

• Managing and overseeing complex fitout projects from start to finish. In addition to the responsibilities of a Fitout Proj
data.jd_text:
Benchmark Fit out is a leading Fit out company looking for a Projects Manager to handle the projects.

* **Responsibilities**

As Fitout Projects Manager is responsible for :

• Managing and overseeing complex fitout projects from start to finish. In addition to the responsibilities of a Fitout Project Manager.

• Mentoring and leading project teams: As a senior manager, the Fitout Project Manager may be responsible for mentoring and leading junior project managers and project teams to ensure that projects are delivered successfully.

• Developing and implementing project management processes: The Senior Fitout Project Manager may be responsible for developing and implementing project management processes and best practices to ensure that projects are managed effectively and efficiently.

• Developing and maintaining relationships with clients and stakeholders: The Senior Fitout Project Manager must be able to develop and maintain strong relationships with clients, stakeholders, and other professionals in the industry to ensure that projects are delivered to their satisfaction.

• Identifying and managing complex project risks: The Senior Fitout Project Manager must have the ability to identify and manage complex project risks and to implement contingency plans to minimize their impact on the project.

• Ensuring compliance with industry standards and regulations: The Senior Fitout Project Manager must ensure that all projects are completed in compliance with industry standards and regulations, and that all safety, quality, and environmental considerations are taken into account.

• In summary, a Senior Fitout Project Manager is a highly experienced and skilled professional who has a deep understanding of the design and construction process.

They are responsible for managing complex fitout projects and leading project teams to deliver projects successfully. They must have excellent project management skills, strong leadership abilities, and the ability to build and maintain relationships with clients and stakeholders

Qualifications

• Bachelor's Degree of Architectural or equivalent experience

• 10 year experience on similar role

• Strong verbal, written, and organizational skills

data.title: Project Manager - Construction (Remediation)
data.raw_snippet:
**Building on our past. Ready for the future**
Worley is a global professional services company of energy, chemicals and resources experts. We partner with customers to deliver projects and create value over the life of their assets. We’re bridging two worlds, moving towards more sustainable energy
data.jd_text:
**Building on our past. Ready for the future**
Worley is a global professional services company of energy, chemicals and resources experts. We partner with customers to deliver projects and create value over the life of their assets. We’re bridging two worlds, moving towards more sustainable energy sources, while helping to provide the energy, chemicals and resources needed now.
  
  
**About The Job**
We’re looking for a Leader, Construction / Construction Project Manager to join our Ahmadi, Kuwait team. Be a part of Worley's dynamic team as we extend our management of the KERP program for another five years. With million m2 of land successfully cleared from contamination and million tons remediated, we're leading the charge in sustainable environmental restoration. Join us and contribute to this incredible journey of transforming contaminated sites into thriving ecosystems.
  
  
When you work for us, you get the chance to join nearly 50,000 people all driven by a common purpose – delivering a more sustainable world. We’re excited about the challenges ahead. If you ask us, there’s never been a more rewarding time to work in the energy, chemical and resources sectors.
  
  
**What You'll Do**

* Management, control, coordination and supervision services as well as assistance for the assigned project inclusive of budget formulation, cost control and quality assurance.
* Management of support services for supervising and directing engineering, procurement and management of construction contractors appointed by the Client.
* Management of services for Oil & Gas production activities including start-up and initial operations assistance as well as operational personnel and systems as required by the Client.
* Reports to the Line Manager and is authorized to deputize for him during his absence with a proven track record of managing multi-projects in general and Design & Engineering and Construction function in particular, in the oil and gas industry
* Is responsible for developing the overall strategy for soil remediation in the assigned areas, it includes contracting strategies and management of contractors.
* Manage and coordinate multidiscipline engineering activities to ensure projects such as FEED (front end engineering design) studies and preparation, ITB (invitation to bid) check estimate preparation, LSTK (lump sum turnkey) tender package preparation are executed in a safe, efficient, cost effective and timely manner.
* Act as primary technical coordination interface with client to ensure that the client’s expectations are satisfied.
* Ensure the project conforms to the client’s approved Basis of Design and all applicable regulatory and code requirements.
* We’re looking for a Leader, Construction / Construction Project Manager to join our Ahmadi, Kuwait team. Be a part of Worley's dynamic team as we extend our management of the KERP program for another five years. With million m2 of land successfully cleared from contamination and million tons remediated, we're leading the charge in sustainable environmental restoration. Join us and contribute to this incredible journey of transforming contaminated sites into thriving ecosystems.
* When you work for us, you get the chance to join nearly 50,000 people all driven by a common purpose – delivering a more sustainable world. We’re excited about the challenges ahead. If you ask us, there’s never been a more rewarding time to work in the energy, chemical and resources sector

**What You'll Have**
**HSE Management**

* Maintains HSE focus within the team to ensure OHSE requirements included in design and procurement.

**Client Relationships**

* Maintains and builds healthy client relationships.
* Proactively resolves performance, scope and schedule issues with the Client as they arises
* Identifies, documents, communicates and meets the client’s requirements

**Scope Management**

* Compliance with WPMP (Worley Project Management Plan)
* Compliance with requirements of the contract.
* Effective change control process application.
* Achievement of claims mitigation and claims management if appropriate.

**Cost Control**

* Monitors cost aspects of the project within direct control to forecast final costs, identifies trends and takes action to avoid cost overruns.
* Promotes implementation of VIP (value improving practices) on the assigned project to achieve overall effectiveness of the use of capital on projects.

**Quality Management**

* Contributes to compliance with Worley policies and procedures.
* Strong working knowledge of Worley engineering and procurement systems and procedures.

**Team Management**

* Focuses team on achievement of milestones and other key project objectives.
* Listens to the team, discusses the issues, resolves problems and directs activities to remove roadblocks and achieve objectives.

**Design Management**

* Sets clear objectives to the design team, including achieving productivity and budget targets.
* Ensures design team aware of, and complies with, applicable regulations and codes.
* Manages inter-discipline interfaces effectively.

**About You**
You will have Bachelor's degree in Engineering with a remarkable year of experience.
  
  

* Remarkable year of experience.
* You are an individual who is self-motivated, proactive, and works well with a team.
* You will be seen as the Subject matter specialist for the resolution of issues arising across all areas of the job and be able to clearly explain complex processes that surround the role.
* We expect you to ensure the integrity of the work scope and that all work is carried-out in accordance with applicable codes and standards.
* We expect you to mentor of less experienced members of staff including ‘hands on’ approach to the formulation and delivery of training programs for local Kuwaiti personnel.

**Why you should apply**

* Providing an agile culture and challenging work opportunities results in a positive work environment. Our people enjoy competitive compensation packages and a fun, personal, collaborative and safe working environment. We value equal employment opportunity and are committed to promoting fairness, equality and diversity.
* Our policy is to conduct background checks for all candidates who accept an offer of employment with us.

**Moving forward together**
We’re committed to building a diverse, inclusive and respectful workplace where everyone feels they belong, can bring themselves, and are heard. We provide equal employment opportunities to all qualified applicants and employees without regard to age, race, creed, color, religion, sex, national origin, ancestry, disability status, veteran status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetic information, marital status, citizenship status or any other basis as protected by law.
  
  
We want our people to be energized and empowered to drive sustainable impact. So, our focus is on a values-inspired culture that unlocks brilliance through belonging, connection and innovation.
  
  
And we're not just talking about it; we're doing it. We're reskilling our people, leveraging transferable skills, and supporting the transition of our workforce to become experts in today's low carbon energy infrastructure and technology.
  
  
Whatever your ambition, there’s a path for you here. And there’s no barrier to your potential career success. Join us to broaden your horizons, explore diverse opportunities, and be part of delivering sustainable change.

data.title: Technical Project Manager
data.raw_snippet:
**Drive Delivery. Create Clarity. Keep Teams Moving Forward.**

Our IT team is looking for a Technical Project Manager who thrives at the intersection of product, engineering, and delivery. This role is critical to ensuring complex technical initiatives move from idea to execution with clarity, pred
data.jd_text:
**Drive Delivery. Create Clarity. Keep Teams Moving Forward.**

Our IT team is looking for a Technical Project Manager who thrives at the intersection of product, engineering, and delivery. This role is critical to ensuring complex technical initiatives move from idea to execution with clarity, predictability, and momentum.

You will work closely with Product Managers, Engineering Managers, and Tech Leads to translate requirements into actionable plans, keep delivery on track, and remove obstacles before they become risks. If you are energized by ownership, execution, and helping teams deliver consistently in fast-moving environments, this role is for you.

**What You’ll Be Doing:**

**Delivery Planning & Execution**

* Partner with Product Managers and Engineering Managers to translate BRDs and requirements into clear, executable delivery plans.
* Define and maintain realistic timelines, milestones, and schedules across multiple teams and systems.
* Track day-to-day progress, ensuring tasks move forward and following up with owners to prevent delays.
* Own delivery accountability by clearly answering:
  *Are we on track, and if not, what are we doing about it?*

**Cross-Team Coordination & Risk Management**

* Manage dependencies across frontend, backend, ERP, integrations, infrastructure, and supporting systems.
* Identify, track, and escalate risks, blockers, and delivery threats early, while driving mitigation options.
* Support Engineering Managers in protecting team focus and managing scope changes during sprints and delivery cycles.
* Monitor work-in-progress, bottlenecks, and context switching to improve delivery flow and predictability.

**Agile Delivery & Process Excellence**

* Plan and facilitate core agile ceremonies including sprint planning, daily stand-ups, reviews/demos, and retrospectives.
* Ensure Jira boards remain accurate, transparent, and reliable, with clearly defined ownership and deliverable work items.
* Break down work into testable, incremental deliverables that support smooth development and QA cycles.
* Help teams improve estimation, planning discipline, and delivery predictability over time.
* Drive continuous improvement across the SDLC by identifying friction points and implementing practical, value-driven process enhancements.

**Stakeholder Communication**

* Provide concise, structured status updates to stakeholders, highlighting progress, risks, decisions needed, and timeline impact.
* Ensure leadership has clear visibility into delivery health without unnecessary noise or ambiguity.

**What You Bring to the Table:**

* 2–5 years of experience as a
  Technical Project Manager, Delivery Manager, or similar role in a technology-driven environment.
* Strong understanding of software development lifecycles and Agile delivery practices.
* Experience working with cross-functional technical teams across multiple systems and platforms.
* Hands-on experience using Jira (or similar tools) to manage delivery and execution.
* Strong organizational skills with the ability to manage multiple streams of work simultaneously.
* Clear, confident communication skills with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.
* A proactive mindset focused on problem-solving, accountability, and continuous improvement.

  5-8 years

**Role Overview:**

Our client, a global leader in the Cards & Payments industry, is engaged with a leading bank in Kuwait and is seeking to onboard a
**Loyalty Program Manager.**
The consultant will act as a strategic advisor and trusted partner to the client, ensuring they achieve measurable business outcomes through their loyalty and customer engagement platform.

The role will drive platform adoption, deliver tangible value, and foster long-term relationships that lead to improved customer engagement, retention, and growth. The ideal candidate will also serve as a connecting link between client stakeholders and internal teams, integrating marketing, CRM, loyalty, analytics, and operations to ensure that expectations, SLAs, and strategic objectives are consistently met.

**Key Responsibilities:**

**Drive Adoption & Value**

* Evangelize the loyalty and customer engagement platform with client stakeholders.
* Monitor platform usage, performance and customer behavior, and proactively recommend strategies to improve engagement, retention, and ROI.

**Relationship Management**
.

* Serve as the primary point of contact for assigned accounts.
* Build trusted, long‑term relationships with stakeholders across Marketing, CRM, Loyalty, and Executive teams.

**Strategic Guidance**

* Provide best practices, playbooks, and use cases for loyalty program success, grounded in data and leading market standards.
* Translate data‑driven insights into clear recommendations for the client’s loyalty and customer engagement strategy.

**Program Governance**

* Lead and coordinate regular business reviews (e.g., QBRs) and status updates via calls, presentations, and onsite meetings.
* Align and coordinate internal resources (e.g., product, analytics, operations) to meet client objectives and SLAs.

**Growth & Retention**

* Identify opportunities for account expansion and cross‑sell scheme solutions where appropriate.
* Drive renewal readiness, ensuring high levels of customer satisfaction and relationship health.

**Internal Collaboration & Alignment**

* Work closely with internal product, analytics, and operations teams to address client feedback and prioritize roadmap items that enhance program performance.
* Ensure that client requirements and outcomes are clearly documented and communicated internally.

**Requirements:**

* 5–8 years in customer success, account management, or consulting for enterprise SaaS solutions.
* Experience working on loyalty, CRM, marketing or customer engagement programs
* Strong exposure to commercial models and the ability to connect program performance to business outcomes.
* Proven track record in driving client adoption of platforms or SaaS solutions and delivering measurable business value.
* Experience managing multiple client stakeholders (including Marketing, CRM, Loyalty and Executive teams).
* Familiarity with marketing technology or fintech ecosystems, including APIs, integrations, CRM, customer engagement and loyalty solutions, analytics tools, and customer data platforms.
* High comfort in working with analytics dashboards, interpreting data, and turning insights into concrete actions and recommendations.
* Strong commercial acumen and ability to link loyalty program performance to financial and strategic outcomes.
* Advanced analytical and problem‑solving skills (both quantitative and qualitative).
* Cross‑functional leadership skills to align product, analytics, and operations around client objectives.
* High level of organization, discipline, and follow‑through for governance, QBRs, and action tracking.

data.title: Commercial Project Manager
data.raw_snippet:
**A Snapshot of Your Day**
As a Commercial Project Manager you will be at the forefront of driving commercial project management, handling financial, legal, and compliance aspects to ensure seamless project execution. Your work will significantly impact the team, clients, and partners by ensuring pr
data.jd_text:
**A Snapshot of Your Day**
As a Commercial Project Manager you will be at the forefront of driving commercial project management, handling financial, legal, and compliance aspects to ensure seamless project execution. Your work will significantly impact the team, clients, and partners by ensuring projects meet defined standards and customer satisfaction, aligning with Siemens Energy’s mission of delivering sustainable energy solutions. Expect a collaborative team culture that values professional partnerships, integrity, and excellence, fostering an environment where your contributions are recognized and valued.
  
  
**How You’ll Make An Impact**

* Empower yourself with signature authorization and project target agreements to manage projects as a commercial entrepreneur, focusing on C and/or B categorized projects.
* Collaborate with Project Managers to achieve project targets in cost, time, and quality, ensuring customer satisfaction.
* Coordinate with internal and external stakeholders, including customers and departments, to maintain project alignment and success.
* Ensure accurate cost and pricing calculations, manage project financials, and record them in systems like SAP.
* Oversee financial KPIs, including orders, revenue, costs, profit, and cash flows, adhering to Siemens Energy Financial Reporting Guidelines.
* Manage project receivables, collections, supplier payments, and financial instruments like bank guarantees and letters of credit.

**What You Bring**

* A qualification in Business Administration or Finance, with 5-8 years of experience as a CPM; certification as a ‘C’ CPM is preferred or willingness to obtain it.
* Experience in turnkey/EPC solution business is advantageous, demonstrating attention to quality and commitment to high-quality results.
* An analytical mindset capable of analyzing complex data and deriving insights to support decision-making and project control.
* Strong organizational skills to manage multiple projects, prioritize tasks, and meet deadlines effectively.
* Excellent communication skills for effective collaboration with team members and stakeholders, building strong relationships.
* A proactive attitude, taking initiative to anticipate challenges and propose solutions, fostering team collaboration and achieving collective goals.

**About The Team**
Our Grid Technology division enables a reliable, sustainable, and digital grid. The power grid is the backbone of the energy transition. Siemens Energy offers a leading portfolio and solutions in HVDC transmission, grid stabilization and storage, high voltage switchgears and transformers, and digital grid technology.
  
  
**Who is Siemens Energy?**
At Siemens Energy, we are more than just an energy technology company. With ~100,000 dedicated employees in more than 90 countries, we develop the energy systems of the future, ensuring that the growing energy demand of the global community is met reliably and sustainably. The technologies created in our research departments and factories drive the energy transition and provide the base for one sixth of the world's electricity generation.
  
  
Our global team is committed to making sustainable, reliable, and affordable energy a reality by pushing the boundaries of what is possible. We uphold a 150-year legacy of innovation that encourages our search for people who will support our focus on decarbonization, new technologies, and energy transformation.
  
  
Find out how you can make a difference at Siemens Energy: https://www.siemens-energy.com/employeevideo
  
  
**Our Commitment to Diversity**
Lucky for us, we are not all the same. Through diversity we generate power. We run on inclusion and our combined creative energy is fueled by over 130 nationalities. Siemens Energy celebrates character – no matter what ethnic background, gender, age, religion, identity, or disability. We energize society, all of society, and we do not discriminate based on our differences.
  
  
**Rewards/Benefits**

* Opportunities to work with a global team
* Opportunities to work on and lead a variety of innovative projects
* Continual learning through the Learn@Siemens-Energy platform

https://jobs.siemens-energy.com/jobs

**Behavioral**

* Drives Achievements

data.title: Operations Director
data.raw_snippet:
**Company Description**

Integrated Gas Company (IGC) plays a central role in Oman’s gas sector as the national aggregator and system operator, responsible for optimizing the allocation, management, and commercialization of natural gas resources. Established to enhance efficiency, transparency, and
data.jd_text:
**Company Description**

Integrated Gas Company (IGC) plays a central role in Oman’s gas sector as the national aggregator and system operator, responsible for optimizing the allocation, management, and commercialization of natural gas resources. Established to enhance efficiency, transparency, and value creation, IGC ensures alignment with national energy priorities and economic objectives under Oman Vision 2040.

Through robust governance, strategic coordination across the value chain, and a focus on sustainable growth, IGC enables industrial development, strengthens energy security, and maximizes value for the national economy.

**Role Description**

This is a full-time, on-site role located in Muscat for an Operations Director. The Operations Director leads the planning and execution of IGC’s operational activities, overseeing key functions including capacity booking, nominations, intra-day balancing, emergency management, and settlement. The role works closely with the Transmission System Operator (TSO) to ensure efficient, compliant, and reliable gas commercialization from producers to end users.

This position is critical to ensuring operational excellence, effective shipper performance, and accurate settlement processes, while maintaining strong coordination across internal and external stakeholders.

**Key Responsibilities**

**Strategic & Governance :**

* Support the development and execution of the Operations business plan and performance scorecard
* Contribute to budgeting, forecasting, and cost control
* Monitor operational risks and maintain the risk register
* Consolidate and review operational performance reporting

**Operations Management :**

* Lead day-to-day shipper operations and ensure optimal gas dispatch in coordination with the TSO
* Oversee capacity booking, nominations, re-nominations, and intra-day balancing
* Coordinate emergency response activities and operational continuity
* Act as the key interface with the TSO, ensuring effective communication and alignment
* Supervise settlement processes, including validation of volumes, consumption data, and invoicing
* Support execution and management of shipper and inter-shipper agreements
* Escalate key operational issues and corrective actions to senior management

**Network Expansion Studies and Optimization:**

* Conduct a comprehensive review and analysis of the capacity expansion study, encompassing both technical and commercial aspects, in collaboration with the Transmission System Operator (TSO). This includes providing clear justifications for the project, determining sizing, tariff considerations, capital expenditure (capex), and establishing a timeline.
* Conduct and lead studies to optimize the monetization of molecules in collaboration with key stakeholders. This includes exploring options such as swapping, identifying final destinations, freeing up gas through renewable energy initiatives, and optimizing gas utilization.
* Pursue initiatives and conduct studies, as needed, focusing on the network, end-users, and suppliers to maximize value across the gas value chain.

**Contract Datasheet and Gas Nomination exercise:**

* Lead and work closely with the Transmission System Operator (TSO) to prepare contract datasheets in alignment with the Transportation Code, ensuring sufficient system capacity is secured.
* Oversee the preparation of next year’s gas nominations and maintenance schedules in collaboration with TSO, suppliers and end-users, ensuring alignment with the Gas Supply Agreement and maximizing margins.
* Coordinate closely with the Planning and Commercial teams to ensure nominations are accurate and meet customer requirements.

**Metering & Validation exercise:**

* Oversee system mass balance (daily, monthly, yearly), ensuring alignment of supply, demand, and linepack
* Validate metering data against invoiced volumes and resolve discrepancies
* Lead metering disputes and mismeasurement resolution with TSO and stakeholders
* Review and approve metering reports and participate in verification and calibration processes.

**Functional Oversight**

* Manage capacity booking processes and maintain accurate records
* Oversee short-term forecasting and nomination submissions
* Monitor gas flows and implement balancing actions
* Ensure effective handling of operational disruptions
* Oversee validation of gas consumption and financial settlements

**Key Interactions**

* Internal: Commercial, Finance, Planning
* External: TSO, contractors, vendors, suppliers, end-users, government entities

Work Environment

Dynamic office environment with moderate to intensive computer use

May require extended hours based on operational needs

**Qualifications & Experience**

* 15+ years of relevant experience, including at least 5 years in engineering and natural gas operation.
* Deep understanding of Oman’s gas network system, gas regulatory framework, and sector operations.
* Experience in the gas business includes gas conditioning, processing, transportation, extraction, fractioning, and liquification.
* Experience in Engineering work including feasibility, concept engineering and FEED.
* Relevant experience in network operations considering factors such as product type, quantity, destination, and transportation mode.
* Expertise in monitoring performance metrics, data validation, cash flow analysis, and stakeholder coordination.
* Proven ability to lead cross-functional teams and drive operational excellence in a complex regulatory environment.

Why Join Us:

At IGC, you will play a central role in shaping the future of Oman’s energy sector. This is a rare opportunity to lead a high-impact function, influence national energy strategy, and create sustainable value for the Sultanate and its people.

**This is a 4 Year Contract**

Application Deadline :
**26 April 2026**

Only eligible candidates will be contacted.

QNB Group’s presence through its subsidiaries and associate companies extends to more than 31 countries acr
data.jd_text:
**About QNB**
Established in 1964 as the country’s first Qatari-owned commercial bank, QNB Group has steadily grown to become the largest bank in the Middle East and Africa (MEA) region.
  
  
QNB Group’s presence through its subsidiaries and associate companies extends to more than 31 countries across three continents providing a comprehensive range of advanced products and services. The total number of employees is more than 28,000 serving up to 20 million customers operating through 1,000 locations, with an ATM network of 4,300 machines.
  
  
QNB has maintained its position as one of the highest rated regional banks from leading credit rating agencies including Standard & Poor’s (A), Moody’s (Aa3) and Fitch (A+). The Bank has also been the recipient of many awards from leading international specialised financial publications.
  
  
Based on the Group’s consistent strong financial performance and its expanding international presence, QNB currently ranks as the most valuable bank brand in the Middle East and Africa, according to Brand Finance Magazine.
  
  
QNB Group has an active community support program and sponsors various social, educational and sporting events.
  
  
**Job Purpose Summary**
The incumbent will lead in a proactive and prudent fashion the Bank’s treasury function by managing the Bank’s cash flows, asset – liability profile and foreign exchange exposures/risk and generate supplementary income within the set strategy, risk appetite, applicable regulatory limits and approved products.
  
  
Manages activity of the trading team by implementing the defined Group Risk Management Framework in which they operate. Develops and actively pursues cross-border trading opportunities and developing a strategy to grow the business. Has thorough knowledge of a broad range of trading techniques including trading/hedging strategies (stack and roll, cross commodity), market liquidity and volatility, options, derivatives, futures, swaps, exotics pricing and trading, and has broad understanding of commodity markets. Has extensive background in risk management strategies and is responsible for ensuring that overall value and risk exposure of the trading portfolio remains within the agreed Group strategy.
  
  
**Essential Duties & Responsibilities By Dimensions**

* Oversees the development and implementation of annual business plans and target budgets for units of responsibility with emphasis on achievement of set targets/ objectives, business growth and expansion; in accordance with the Bank’s overall budget and strategy.
* Act within the limits of the powers delegated to the incumbent and delegate activities/ authority to the respective staff as required and monitor exercise of the same.
* Approves formulated, and/or specific strategies which cover balance sheet asset, liability and contingent allocation, longer term funding arrangements asset diversification, use of market products and financial instruments with risks identified, and hedging, trading and investment strategies.
* Oversees the implementation and evaluation of the Treasury strategic and business development plans and achievement of related short and long term business goals both locally and internationally.
* Approves Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) created for the purpose of performance monitoring and quality measurement purposes for the Treasury function and monitor their achievements on periodic basis.
* Implements KPI’s and best practices for role
* Promote cost consciousness and efficiency and enhance productivity, to minimise cost, avoid waste, and optimise benefits for the bank.
* Demonstrate clear understanding of the important factors behind the bank's financial & non-financial performance.
* Approves Service Level Agreements (SLAs) with internal departments/ units to achieve improvements in turnaround time (TAT) which will also help increase external customer satisfaction.
* Provide timely and accurate information to the external and internal auditors, the Compliance function, and Group Risk as and when required.
* Meets with visiting banks representatives, and maintains credit lines and services through active relationship management.
* Establishes and oversees the management of relationships with key internal and external stakeholders and managing key client interface.
* Provide feedback to Corporate/Retail Banking business units’ managers and, as appropriate, recommend course of action.
* To assist customers in all their queries on Bank’s product and seek solution to their requests
* Maintain activities in accordance with Service Level Agreements (SLAs) with internal departments/ units to achieve improvements in turn-around time.
* Build and maintain strong and effective relationship with all other related departments and units to achieve the Group’s goals/objectives.
* Provide timely and accurate information to the external and internal auditors and the Compliance function as and when required.

**Education/Experience Requirements**

* University graduate preferably with a Major in Finance, Banking, Economics, or Mathematics (related field of study), Masters preferred.
* MBA, CFA, ACT or related professional certification is preferable.
* 15 years diverse Treasury experience, of which 5 years must have been in a leading role in a Treasury Division, preferably in the country or with an international bank.
* Additional commercial banking experience, especially credit and market risk management would be of value.

Note: you will be required to attach the following:
  
  

* Resume/CV

data.jd_text:
**Vice President Global Corporate Coverage - Omani National -**
**36550**
Some careers prize diversity more than others.
  
  
If you’re looking for further opportunities to develop your career, take the next step in fulfilling your potential right here at HSBC.
  
  
**Role Purpose**
Managing a portfolio of clients across multiple sectors of key CIB Banking Relationships, mainly Government Related Entities, within the Oman coverage team . To provide pro-active support in deepening client relationships and improving income generation through focused business development activities, including managing the portfolio of clients independently.
  
  
**In This Role, You Will**

* Contribute to the continued development of the CIB Banking and specifically sector strategy. The client base comprises some of the country leading in various sectors such as Oil & Gas, Chemicals, Shipping, Utilities Providers, Telco, Aviation, to name few.
* Ensure prom pt and appropriate response to customer enquiries and concerns by problem solving and facilitating the process by liaising with product and support areas
* Work with line manager/ Head of Banking in identifying strategies to maintain /enhance overall relationship return, increase revenues while optimizing the utilization of credit facilities, and ensure early identification of potential credit issues
* Provide general support to the Line Manager/Head of Banking in servicing client relationships and enhancing income generation.
* Manage and develop a portfolio of own clients by deepening client relationships and improving income generation through focused business development activities
* Work with the Business Credit Office on the preparation of structured and focused credit memorandums for clients within the sectors, covering annual reviews and new money proposals
* Understand and ensure compliance with all relevant internal and external rules, regulations and procedures that apply to the conduct of the business in which the jobholder is involved.
* Maintain HSBC internal control standards, including timely implementation of internal and external audit points together with any issues raised by external regulators.
* Develop strong sectorial/product expertise and provide high quality competitor and client level analysis to support business development and credit risk assessment
* Undertake all required activities as directed by line manager/Head of Banking, or as agreed in annual objectives, in support of achieving the business objectives CIB Banking
* Liaise with members of the client service team, ensuring a co-ordinated approach to enhancing the client relationship.
* Engage with relevant product teams including GPS, GTS, FX, etc to ensure coordinated approach towards achieving relationship development objectives.
* Develop strong bonds with the decision makers of your own client portfolio
* Work diligently with Credit Risk Management to present written recommendations and manage risk
* Proactively and effectively manage financial crime risk in team portfolio. Work closely with FCC to resolve all queries and issues that might arise from time to time.
* Co-ordinate closely with the Banking Middle Office (BMO) to ensure new account on-boarding queries are addressed quickly and the on-boarding process is completed in a timely manner.
* Live and represent the HSBC Brand and Values both internally and externally
* Uphold the highest level of integrity and act as a role model for all HSBC values and business principles.
* Take an active role in initiatives outside of own role; proactively seeking different assignments to accelerate learning.
* Establish strong relationships with internal stakeholders.
* Manage Deals and Pipeline transactions within ClientVision to ensure that the transaction data and status are accurately reflected
* Monitor income generation and effectively manage the financial reporting and analysis for the client portfolio

**To be successful in the role, you should meet the following requirements:**

* Understanding of risk management and credit mitigants
* Strong business development, financial analysis and analytical skills
* Ability to undertake in depth review of industry, competitors, clients and products and provide a high-quality analysis of Credit risk
* Ability to develop good working partnerships with credit, client management and product teams
* Ability to provide strong analytical and marketing support to Relationship Managers
* Strong IT skills (including Word, Excel, PowerPoint etc)
* Knowledge of the full spectrum of CIB products and services
* Robust understanding of Financial Crime Risk Management
* Excellent verbal and written communication skills
* Strong team player and demonstrate a high level of attention to details, strong organizational skills and willingness to be a driven self-starter

You’ll achieve more at HSBC.
  
  
HSBC is committed to building a culture where all employees are valued, respected and opinions count. We take pride in providing a workplace that fosters continuous professional development, flexible working and opportunities to grow within an inclusive and diverse environment.
  
  
We believe diversity brings benefits for our customers, our business and our people. Different ideas and perspectives help us innovate, manage risk and grow the business in a sustainable way. Personal data held by the Bank relating to employment applications will be used in accordance with our Privacy Statement, which is available on our website.
  
  
Issued by The Hong Kong and Shanghai Banking Corporation Limited.

data.title: PMO Governance & Assurance Manager
data.raw_snippet:
**SUMMARY OF ROLE**

The Assurance & Governance Manager – PMO is responsible for ensuring robust governance, control, and assurance across the Bank’s project and program portfolio. The role oversees adherence to the project management framework, policies, and procedures, ensuring consistent, high-qu
data.jd_text:
**SUMMARY OF ROLE**

The Assurance & Governance Manager – PMO is responsible for ensuring robust governance, control, and assurance across the Bank’s project and program portfolio. The role oversees adherence to the project management framework, policies, and procedures, ensuring consistent, high-quality delivery aligned with time, cost, and quality objectives. The role also drives PMO maturity by conducting health checks, enhancing governance standards, strengthening reporting accuracy, and embedding continuous improvement practices across all projects and programs within the portfolio.

**KEY RESPONSIBILITIES**

**Portfolio Governance & Reporting**

* Lead the preparation and consolidation of weekly and monthly portfolio reports, ensuring accuracy, completeness, and alignment with actual project performance.
* Review and challenge inputs from Project Managers to ensure quality, consistency, and transparency of reporting across all governance forums.
* Ensure all reporting is delivered within agreed timelines and meets senior management and governance requirements.

**Project Assurance & Health Checks**

* Conduct periodic project health checks across all programs and projects to assess performance against scope, timeline, cost, and quality.
* Identify gaps, risks, and non-compliance areas, and ensure timely tracking and closure of corrective actions.
* Provide independent assurance on project delivery and escalate critical issues impacting portfolio performance.

**Risk, Issue & Dependency Management**

* Monitor and ensure effective management of risks, issues, and interdependencies across projects.
* Highlight and escalate gaps where risks and issues are not actively managed by project teams.
* Support mitigation planning to minimize impact on project delivery and overall portfolio performance.

**PMO Controls & Registers Management**

* Oversee and maintain PMO registers, including risk logs, change control, contracts, VAPT exceptions, and lessons learned.
* Ensure registers are consistently updated, accurate, and aligned with governance requirements.
* Implement standardized processes for tracking and reporting across all PMO control areas.

**Governance Forums & Stakeholder Coordination**

* Plan, coordinate, and facilitate governance forums including project boards and steering committees.
* Ensure accurate documentation of meeting minutes, decisions, and actions within defined timelines.
* Track and follow up on all actions to ensure timely closure and accountability.

**Financial Governance & Budget Oversight**

* Establish and monitor project budget tracking and forecasting processes aligned with approved plans.
* Ensure financial data is accurately reflected in portfolio reporting and governance discussions.

**Post-Implementation Review & Continuous Improvement**

* Review and quality assure post-implementation reviews to ensure alignment between planned and actual outcomes.
* Ensure lessons learned are captured, documented, and shared across the PMO.
* Conduct quarterly analysis of lessons learned to identify trends and drive continuous improvement

**Process Improvement & PMO Maturity**

* Identify opportunities to enhance PMO processes, tools, and governance frameworks.
* Lead initiatives to improve PMO maturity, efficiency, and effectiveness.
* Promote best practices in project management and governance across the organization.

**Stakeholder Management & Collaboration**

* Build and maintain strong working relationships with project sponsors, business units, IT, and senior stakeholders.
* Influence stakeholders to ensure adherence to governance standards and successful project delivery.
* Provide guidance and support to Project Managers on governance, reporting, and assurance requirements.

Requirements
  
  

· Omani National

· Bachelor’s degree in Business Administration, Project Management, or a related field

· Minimum 5–7 years of experience in project management, PMO, or governance roles

* ***Communication and Interpersonal Skills: Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to interact effectively w
data.jd_text:
***Skills :***

* ***Organizational and Time Management Skills: Ability to prioritize tasks, meet deadlines, and manage multiple responsibilities effectively.***
* ***Communication and Interpersonal Skills: Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to interact effectively with diverse stakeholders.***
* ***Attention to Detail: Meticulous attention to detail and accuracy in all tasks.***
* ***Proficiency in Project Management Tools: Familiarity with project management software, such as Microsoft Project, Jira, or Asana.***
* ***Technical Aptitude: Basic understanding of IT concepts and technologies.***

***Problem Solving and Analytical Skills: Ability to identify and troubleshoot problems effectively.***

***Responsibilities:***

***1. Administrative Support:***

* ***Schedule and Coordinate Meetings: Arrange and manage meetings, including scheduling, preparing agendas, and taking minutes.***
* ***Maintain Project Documentation: Organize and update project documents, such as plans, reports, presentations, and meeting minutes.***
* ***Track Project Budget: Assist in tracking project expenses, monitoring budget variances, and preparing budget reports.***
* ***Manage Project Communications: Help facilitate communication between the project team, stakeholders, and other departments.***

***2. Project Planning & Execution Support:***

* ***Assist in Project Planning: Contribute to the development of project plans, including timelines, resource allocation, and risk assessments.***
* ***Support Task Assignments: Help assign tasks to team members and track their progress.***
* ***Coordinate Resources: Assist in the procurement and allocation of necessary resources, such as equipment, software, and personnel.***
* ***Monitor Project Progress: Help track project progress against the plan and identify potential roadblocks.***

***3. Issue & Risk Management Support:***

* ***Identify and Log Issues: Help identify and log project issues and risks.***
* ***Assist in Risk Mitigation: Support the implementation of risk mitigation plans.***
* ***Track and Resolve Issues: Help track and resolve project issues in a timely manner.***

***4. Quality Assurance Support:***

* ***Assist in Quality Control: Help ensure project deliverables meet quality standards.***
* ***Support Testing Activities: Assist in coordinating and conducting testing activities.***

***5. Stakeholder Management Support:***

* ***Maintain Stakeholder Relationships: Help build and maintain positive relationships with stakeholders.***
* ***Communicate Project Updates: Assist in communicating project updates and status reports to stakeholders.***

data.jd_text:
**Technology & Transformation | Cyber | Project Manager – Senior Consultant**
When you work for us, you commit to a career at one of the largest and most prestigious professional services firms in the world. We have received numerous awards over the last few years, including Best Employer in the Middle East, and Best Consulting Firm, and the Middle East Training & Development Excellence Award.
  
  
Deloitte Consulting in the Middle East (“DME-C”) is on an existing transformation and growth journey, as part of this, it plans to further strengthen its internal functions which provide foundations for scaling safely. The journey will pivot us to large and complex deals in transformation; technology implement and operate. Accordingly, DME-C is seeking to recruit a dynamic and experienced
**Project Manager**
**Our Purpose**
Deloitte makes an impact that matters. Every day we challenge ourselves to do what matters most—for clients, for our people, and for society. We serve clients distinctively, bringing innovative insights, solving complex challenges and unlocking sustainable growth. We inspire our talented professionals to deliver outstanding value to clients, providing an exceptional career experience and an inclusive and collaborative culture. We contribute to society, building confidence and trust in the markets, upholding the integrity of organizations and supporting our communities.
  
  
Our shared values guide the way we behave to make a positive, enduring impact:
  
  

* Integrity
* Outstanding value to markets and clients
* Commitment to each other
* Strength from cultural diversity

**Roles And Responsibilities**
Develop detailed project plans, including scope, schedule, resources, and budget.
  
  
Coordinate internal resources and third parties/vendors for the flawless execution of projects.
  
  
Ensure resource availability and allocation.
  
  
Manage changes to the project scope, project schedule, and project costs using appropriate verification techniques.
  
  
Measure project performance using appropriate tools and techniques to monitor progress and identify areas for improvement.
  
  
Report and escalate to management as needed.
  
  
Successfully manage the relationship with the client and all stakeholders.
  
  
**Skills**
Proven working experience in project management.
  
  
Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  
  
Solid organizational skills including attention to detail and multitasking abilities.
  
  
Strong leadership qualities and ability to manage teams.
  
  
Proficiency with project management software tools (e.g., MS Project, JIRA, Asana).
  
  
Bachelor’s degree in business administration, Management, or related field; Project Management Professional (PMP) certification is a plus.
